# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Cluster with Azure Integration

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster/commits)


Terraform module for provisioning MongoDB Atlas clusters with Azure provider integration.
Wraps the generic [`terraform-module-mongoatlas-cluster`](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-cluster)
and adds Azure-specific features: Azure Key Vault credential storage and optional CMEK encryption.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module is the Azure-specific wrapper for the generic MongoDB Atlas cluster module.
It handles all Azure provider interactions, including:

- MongoDB Atlas cluster provisioning (delegated to the generic module)
- Azure Key Vault for secure admin credential storage (stores JSON-encoded credentials as a secret)
- Existing Key Vault referenced by name and resource group — the Key Vault must be pre-provisioned
- Automatic subscription data via `data.azurerm_subscription.current`
- Region shortcode mapping for `system_name` construction (via boilerplate `locals.tf`)

The cluster admin credentials are stored in an Azure Key Vault secret and
exposed via the `cluster_key_vault_secret_id` and `cluster_key_vault_secret_name` outputs.

**Important:** Azure region names (e.g. `eastus2`) do NOT map directly to MongoDB Atlas region
names (e.g. `US_EAST_2`). Always set `settings.regions[].region` explicitly using Atlas Azure
region names. See the [Atlas documentation](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/microsoft-azure/)
for the full list.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster/releases).


### Terragrunt Usage

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-azurerm-cluster.git?ref=v1.2.1"
}

inputs = {
  region         = "eastus2"                  # (Required) Azure region for resource naming. Default: ""
  name_prefix    = "mdb"                      # (Optional) Prefix for resource names. Default: ""
  name           = "my-cluster"               # (Optional) Name of the MongoDB Atlas cluster. Default: ""
  project_id     = "your-atlas-project-id"   # (Optional) Atlas project ID. Default: ""
  project_name   = "my-project"              # (Optional) Atlas project name for tagging. Default: ""
  cloud_provider = "AZURE"                   # (Optional) Default cloud provider for backup export/copy: AWS | GCP | AZURE. Default: "AZURE"
  run_hoop       = false                     # (Optional) Run HOOP agent via null_resource. Default: false

  settings = {
    cluster_type:              "REPLICASET"  # (Optional) REPLICASET | SHARDED | GEOSHARDED. Default: "REPLICASET"
    major_version:             7.0           # (Optional) MongoDB major version. Default: null
    termination_protection:    true          # (Optional) Enable termination protection. Default: null
    version_release:           "LTS"         # (Optional) LTS | CONTINUOUS. Default: "LTS"
    encryption_at_rest_enabled: false        # (Optional) Enable encryption at rest. Default: false
    encryption_at_rest_provider: "AZURE"     # (Optional) AWS | GCP | AZURE. Default: "AZURE"
    cloud_provider:            "AZURE"       # (Optional) Override default cloud provider for backup. Default: var.cloud_provider

    admin_user:
      enabled:                  true         # (Optional) Create Atlas admin user. Default: false
      username:                 "admin"      # (Optional) Username. Default: auto-generated
      auth_database:            "admin"      # (Optional) Authentication database. Default: "admin"
      key_vault_name:           "my-kv"     # (Required when enabled) Azure Key Vault name
      key_vault_resource_group: "my-rg"     # (Required when enabled) Key Vault resource group
      use_external_rotation:    false        # (Optional) Use external rotation manager. Default: false
      rotation_lambda_name:     ""           # (Optional) External rotator identifier. Required when use_external_rotation is true.
      rotation_period:          90           # (Optional) Password rotation period in days. Default: 90
      rotation_duration:        "1h"         # (Optional) Duration for external rotator. Default: "1h"
      password_rotation_period: 90           # (Optional) Terraform-managed rotation period in days. Default: 90

    regions:
      - provider:          "AZURE"           # (Optional) TENANT | AWS | GCP | AZURE. Default: "TENANT"
        backing_provider:  "AZURE"           # (Optional) Backing provider for TENANT clusters. Default: null
        region:            "US_EAST_2"       # (Required) Atlas Azure region name — must be set explicitly
        priority:          7                 # (Optional) Node priority 1-7. Default: 7
        electable:
          size:      "M10"                   # (Optional) Instance size. Default: "M2"
          count:     3                       # (Optional) Node count. Default: null
          disk_size: 10                      # (Optional) Disk size in GB. Default: null
        analytics:
          size:      "M10"                   # (Optional) Analytics instance size. Default: "M2"
          count:     1                       # (Optional) Analytics node count. Default: null
          disk_size: 10                      # (Optional) Disk size in GB for analytics. Default: null
        auto_scaling:
          disk:       false                  # (Optional) Enable disk auto-scaling. Default: false
          compute:    false                  # (Optional) Enable compute auto-scaling. Default: false
          max_size:   "M40"                  # (Optional) Max instance size. Default: null
          min_size:   "M10"                  # (Optional) Min instance size. Default: null
          scale_down: false                  # (Optional) Allow scale-down. Default: false

    backup:
      enabled:             true              # (Optional) Enable cloud backup. Default: false
      hour_of_day:         2                 # (Optional) Backup hour 0-23. Default: 0
      restore_window_days: 7                 # (Optional) Restore window in days. Default: 1
      auto_export:         false             # (Optional) Enable automatic export. Default: false
      export_prefix:       ""               # (Optional) Prefix for exported backup files. Default: null
      daily:
        retention_value: 7                   # (Optional) Daily retention in days. Default: 7
      weekly:
        retention_value: 4                   # (Optional) Weekly retention in weeks. Default: 4
      monthly:
        retention_value: 12                  # (Optional) Monthly retention in months. Default: 12
      export:
        cloud_provider: "AZURE"              # (Optional) Override provider for this export. Default: settings.cloud_provider
        service_url:    "https://..."        # (Required for GCP/AZURE) Export bucket URL
        role_id:        "role-id"            # (Required for GCP/AZURE) Role ID for bucket access
        frecuency_type: "DAILY"              # (Optional) HOURLY | DAILY | WEEKLY | MONTHLY | YEARLY. Default: "DAILY"
      copy:
        cloud_provider: "AZURE"              # (Optional) Override provider for copy. Default: settings.cloud_provider
        frequencies:    []                   # (Optional) Frequency types to copy
        region_name:    "US_EAST_2"          # (Optional) Target Atlas region. Default: deployment region
        copy_oplogs:    false                # (Optional) Include oplogs. Default: false

    shards:
      - zone_name: "zone-1"                  # (Optional) Zone name (required for GEOSHARDED). Default: null
        regions:
          - provider:   "AZURE"
            region:     "EUROPE_WEST"
            priority:   7
            electable:
              size:      "M30"
              count:     3
              disk_size: 10

    hoop:
      enabled:        false                  # (Optional) Enable hoop_connections output. Default: false
      community:      true                   # (Optional) true=community (returns null for AzureRM), false=enterprise. Default: true
      agent_id:       ""                     # (Required when enabled+enterprise) Hoop.dev agent UUID
      tags:           {}                     # (Optional) Tags map for the Hoop connection. Default: {}
      access_control: []                     # (Optional) Access control group list. Default: []
      import:         false                  # (Optional) Import existing Hoop connection instead of creating. Default: false
      # Output hoop_connections["admin"].name pattern: "mongo-db-<cluster_name>-ow"
  }
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Configure Azure and MongoDB Atlas credentials:
   ```bash
   az login
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PUBLIC_KEY="your_public_key"
   export MONGODB_ATLAS_PRIVATE_KEY="your_private_key"
   ```

2. Ensure the Azure Key Vault exists and your identity has `Key Vault Secrets Officer` permissions.

3. Create a Terragrunt configuration using the usage example above.

4. Initialize and apply:
   ```bash
   terraform init && terraform apply
   ```

5. Retrieve credentials from Key Vault:
   ```bash
   az keyvault secret show \
     --vault-name my-kv \
     --name $(terraform output -raw cluster_key_vault_secret_name) \
     --query value -o tsv | jq .
   ```


## Examples

### Basic Azure Cluster

```hcl
inputs = {
  region     = "eastus2"
  project_id = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name       = "azure-cluster"

  settings = {
    cluster_type           = "REPLICASET"
    major_version          = 7.0
    termination_protection = true
    admin_user = {
      enabled                  = true
      username                 = "azure-admin"
      key_vault_name           = "prod-kv"
      key_vault_resource_group = "prod-rg"
      password_rotation_period = 90
    }
    regions = [{
      provider         = "AZURE"
      backing_provider = "AZURE"
      region           = "US_EAST_2"
      priority         = 7
      electable = {
        size      = "M10"
        count     = 3
        disk_size = 10
      }
    }]
  }
}
```

### Multi-Region Cluster with Backup and Export

```hcl
inputs = {
  region         = "eastus2"
  project_id     = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name           = "multi-region-cluster"
  cloud_provider = "AZURE"

  settings = {
    cluster_type = "REPLICASET"
    admin_user = {
      enabled                  = true
      key_vault_name           = "prod-kv"
      key_vault_resource_group = "prod-rg"
      use_external_rotation    = false
      password_rotation_period = 90
    }
    regions = [
      {
        provider         = "AZURE"
        backing_provider = "AZURE"
        region           = "US_EAST_2"
        priority         = 7
        electable        = { size = "M10", count = 3, disk_size = 10 }
      },
      {
        provider         = "AZURE"
        backing_provider = "AZURE"
        region           = "US_WEST"
        priority         = 6
        electable        = { size = "M10", count = 2, disk_size = 10 }
      }
    ]
    backup = {
      enabled             = true
      hour_of_day         = 2
      restore_window_days = 7
      auto_export         = true
      daily               = { retention_value = 7 }
      weekly              = { retention_value = 4 }
      monthly             = { retention_value = 12 }
      export = {
        cloud_provider = "AZURE"
        service_url    = "https://myaccount.blob.core.windows.net/backups"
        role_id        = "my-atlas-role-id"
        frecuency_type = "DAILY"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Sharded Cluster

```hcl
inputs = {
  region     = "eastus2"
  project_id = "your-atlas-project-id"
  name       = "sharded-cluster"

  settings = {
    cluster_type = "SHARDED"
    regions = [{
      provider         = "AZURE"
      backing_provider = "AZURE"
      region           = "US_EAST_2"
      priority         = 7
      electable        = { size = "M30", count = 3, disk_size = 10 }
    }]
    shards = [
      {
        zone_name = "shard-1"
        regions = [{
          provider         = "AZURE"
          backing_provider = "AZURE"
          region           = "EUROPE_WEST"
          priority         = 7
          electable        = { size = "M30", count = 3, disk_size = 10 }
        }]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_cloud_provider"></a> [cloud\_provider](#input\_cloud\_provider) | Default cloud provider for Atlas backup export bucket and copy settings. Valid values: 'AWS', 'GCP', 'AZURE'. Can be overridden per-resource in settings. | `string` | `"AZURE"` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | Name of the resource | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | Prefix for the name of the resources | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_project_id"></a> [project\_id](#input\_project\_id) | (optional) The ID of the Atlas project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_project_name"></a> [project\_name](#input\_project\_name) | (optional) The name of the Atlas project where the cluster will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_region"></a> [region](#input\_region) | Azure Region to deploy resources into. Example: 'eastus2', defaults to empty string as some of the resources may not require region setting. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_run_hoop"></a> [run\_hoop](#input\_run\_hoop) | DEPRECATED: No-op. Use the hoop\_connections output with terraform-module-hoop-connection instead. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | Settings for the MongoDB Atlas cluster and Azure integrations | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_cluster_admin_user"></a> [cluster\_admin\_user](#output\_cluster\_admin\_user) | n/a |

| <a name="output_cluster_key_vault_secret_id"></a> [cluster\_key\_vault\_secret\_id](#output\_cluster\_key\_vault\_secret\_id) | Azure Key Vault secret resource ID storing the cluster admin credentials. |
| <a name="output_cluster_key_vault_secret_name"></a> [cluster\_key\_vault\_secret\_name](#output\_cluster\_key\_vault\_secret\_name) | Name of the Azure Key Vault secret storing the cluster admin credentials. |
| <a name="output_cluster_name"></a> [cluster\_name](#output\_cluster\_name) | n/a |
| <a name="output_cluster_server_type"></a> [cluster\_server\_type](#output\_cluster\_server\_type) | n/a |
| <a name="output_cluster_state"></a> [cluster\_state](#output\_cluster\_state) | n/a |
| <a name="output_cluster_version"></a> [cluster\_version](#output\_cluster\_version) | n/a |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definition for the cluster admin user.<br/>Returns null in community mode — Azure Key Vault has no native Hoop community provider.<br/>Enterprise mode: pass directly as the `connections` input to terraform-module-hoop-connection.<br/>Note: terraform-module-hoop-connection manages connections via the Hoop provider; ensure your<br/>Hoop agent has access to retrieve secrets from Azure Key Vault. |
